frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Priya, bura mat maanna beta, padh-likh gayi ho toh achchi baat hai, par shaadi-shuda aurat ki pehchaan bhi honi chahiye. Maang mein thoda sindoor aur gale mein mangalsutra se bahu suhagan lagti hai, ghar ka shagun bana rehta hai. Aajkal ki ladkiyan ise bas choice bol deti hain, lekin hamari parampara ka bhi kuch maan hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames visible marital markers as a married woman's proper identity and duty, using tradition and auspiciousness rather than explicit control. It encodes pressure to wear sindoor/mangalsutra consistently and judges women who reject them as disrespecting culture.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 62. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son and his wife are discussing whether to put your grandchild in a particular school. Your daughter in law Priya has done extensive research Ñ she has visited three schools, spoken to other parents, read reviews, compared curricula. She is presenting her recommendation at the family dinner table. Your son is listening. You are also listening. When Priya finishes you speak. What you say and how you frame your own opinion versus Priya's research reveals the specific mechanism by which women's knowledge and effort is absorbed and then overridden by older family authority.
